what: FCA classification for FSC-2417: HIGH (call-A=HIGH, call-B=HIGH, agreement=True)
why: FCA Risk Classifier ran two independent classification calls to prevent anchoring. Both calls agreed. Triggers: ['Suitability__c', 'SuitabilityScoreCalculator', 'RiskProfile__c', 'FinancialAccount.ConsolidatedSuitabilityScore__c', 'SuitabilityScoreUpdated__e', 'COBS 9.2', 'Consumer Duty PS22/9', 'COBS 3.4', 'MiFID II risk profile', 'suitability assessment'].

data.classification_rationale: FSC-2417 directly modifies and extends the Suitability__c object by introducing a SuitabilityScoreCalculator Apex class triggered on Suitability__c updates, and stores a derived ConsolidatedSuitabilityScore__c on FinancialAccount — placing it squarely within the HIGH-FCA tier under Rule 1. The story explicitly references COBS 9.2 suitability assessment obligations and Consumer Duty PS22/9, and Acceptance Criterion 4 enforces a mandatory COBS 9.2 compliance warning gate before advice can be recorded. RiskProfile__c is also a named input dimension (40% weighting), further anchoring this story to the highest regulatory tier.
regulatory_obligations: Consumer Duty PS22/9 — Good Outcomes for Retail Clients
SYSC — Governance and Oversight of Advice Processes
